political.s: Frontier's political donations show bipartisan lean with Rep% of 64% and Dem% of 36%, indicating a right-leaning tilt overall. The company raised $26 million in FEC donations, balancing donations across both major parties despite the Republican preference.

charity.s: Frontier provides in-kind donations through flight vouchers to nonprofits and raised $395,000 for Folds of Honor in 2023, bringing total giving to that organization to nearly $1 million. However, overall charitable giving appears modest relative to company size, and the company is no longer accepting general donation requests.
charity.focus: Military families (Folds of Honor), disaster relief, healthcare, homeless animal rescue

environment.s: Frontier operates the most fuel-efficient fleet among major U.S. carriers and was awarded North American Environmental Sustainability Airline of the Year in 2024. The company saved 160 million gallons of fuel in 2023 and maintains one of the youngest aircraft fleets with advanced A320neo technology, though net-zero emissions goals and SAF commitments remain limited.

labor.s: Frontier faces significant labor tensions, with flight attendants voting 99.6% to authorize strikes in September 2024 over management's business model changes that reduce compensation and increase commuting costs. The company has also faced wage violation lawsuits regarding overtime pay practices, indicating poor labor relations and worker compensation issues.

dei.s: Frontier maintains DEI commitments with business resource groups and leadership development programs, but has reduced public emphasis on DEI in response to political pressure. Employee ratings show neutral to below-average DEI scores (3.3/5 on Glassdoor), placing them at industry average despite stated initiatives.

privacy.s: Frontier shares customer data with third parties for direct marketing and collects extensive behavioral data including email submissions, travel history, and social media tracking. The company received a Warning rating from Common Sense Privacy, indicating concerns around data selling and profiling practices.

execPay.s: Limited recent executive pay ratio data available; average executive compensation is $229,743 while average employee salary is approximately $86,296, suggesting roughly 2.7:1 ratio. Employees rank compensation in bottom 5% of comparable companies on Comparably, indicating significant pay disparity.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
